v.CNV: 那么，论到在血统（“血统”原文作“肉身”）上作我们祖先的亚伯拉罕所经验的，我们可以说什么呢？
v.ESV: What then shall we say was gained by Abraham, our forefather according to the flesh?
v.GAE: 그런즉 육신으로 우리 조상인 아브라함이 무엇을 얻었다 하리요
v.KJV: What shall we say then that Abraham our father, as pertaining to the flesh, hath found?
v.NASB: What then shall we say that Abraham, our forefather according to the flesh, has found?
v.NIV: What then shall we say that Abraham, our forefather according to the flesh, discovered in this matter?
v.NRSV: What then are we to say was gained by Abraham, our ancestor according to the flesh?
v.SAENEW: 그러면 육신상으로 우리의 조상인 아브라함이 무엇을 얻었다고 우리가 말할 수 있겠습니까?
v.WLB: 그러면 우리가 육신에 따라 우리의 조상이 된 아브라함이 무엇을 얻었다고 말할 수 있겠습니까?

v.CNV: 亚伯拉罕若是因行为称义，就有可夸的，只是不能在　神面前夸口。
v.ESV: For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not before God.
v.GAE: 만일 아브라함이 행위로써 의롭다 하심을 받았으면 자랑할 것이 있으려니와 하나님 앞에서는 없느니라
v.KJV: For if Abraham were justified by works, he hath whereof to glory; but not before God.
v.NASB: For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not before God.
v.NIV: If, in fact, Abraham was justified by works, he had something to boast about—but not before God.
v.NRSV: For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not before God.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함이 행위로 의롭게 되었더라면, 그에게는 자랑할 것이 있었을 것입니다. 그러나 하나님 앞에서는 자랑할 것이 없습니다.
v.WLB: 만일 아브라함이 행위로 의롭다는 인정을 받았다면 자랑할 것이 있겠지만 하나님 앞에서는 없습니다.

v.CNV: 经上怎么样说呢？”亚伯拉罕信　神，这就算为他的义。”
v.ESV: For what does the Scripture say? “Abraham believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness.”
v.GAE: 성경이 무엇을 말하느냐 아브라함이 하나님을 믿으매 그것이 그에게 의로 여겨진 바 되었느니라
v.KJV: For what saith the scripture? Abraham believed God, and it was counted unto him for righteousness.
v.NASB: For what does the Scripture say? 'ABRAHAM BELIEVED GOD, AND IT WAS CREDITED TO HIM AS RIGHTEOUSNESS.'
v.NIV: What does Scripture say? “Abraham believed God, and it was credited to him as righteousness.”
v.NRSV: For what does the scripture say? “Abraham believed God, and it was reckoned to him as righteousness.”
v.SAENEW: 성경이 무엇이라고 말합니까? "아브라함이 하나님을 믿으니, 하나님께서 그를 의롭다고 여기셨다" 하였습니다.
v.WLB: 성경은 무엇이라고 말합니까? “아브라함이 하나님을 믿으니 이것이 그에게 의로 여겨졌다”라고 합니다.

v.CNV: 作工的得工资，不算是恩典，是他应得的。
v.ESV: Now to the one who works, his wages are not counted as a gift but as his due.
v.GAE: 일하는 자에게는 그 삯이 은혜로 여겨지지 아니하고 보수로 여겨지거니와
v.KJV: Now to him that worketh is the reward not reckoned of grace, but of debt.
v.NASB: Now to the one who works, his wage is not credited as a favor, but as what is due.
v.NIV: Now to the one who works, wages are not credited as a gift but as an obligation.
v.NRSV: Now to one who works, wages are not reckoned as a gift but as something due.
v.SAENEW: 일을 하는 사람에게는 품삯을 은혜로 주는 것으로 치지 않고 당연한 보수로 주는 것으로 생각합니다.
v.WLB: 일하는 자에게는 품삯이 은혜로 여겨지지 않고 정당한 대가로 여겨지나

v.CNV: 可是，那不作工而只信那称不敬虔的人为义的　神的，他的信就算为义了。
v.ESV: And to the one who does not work but believes in him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is counted as righteousness,
v.GAE: 일을 아니할지라도 경건하지 아니한 자를 의롭다 하시는 이를 믿는 자에게는 그의 믿음을 의로 여기시나니
v.KJV: But to him that worketh not, but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted for righteousness.
v.NASB: But to the one who does not work, but believes in Him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is credited as righteousness,
v.NIV: However, to the one who does not work but trusts God who justifies the ungodly, their faith is credited as righteousness.
v.NRSV: But to one who without works trusts him who justifies the ungodly, such faith is reckoned as righteousness.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 경건하지 못한 사람을 의롭다고 하시는 분을 믿는 사람은, 비록 아무 공로가 없어도, 그의 믿음이 의롭다고 인정을 받습니다.
v.WLB: 일하지 않고도 경건치 않은 사람을 의롭다고 인정하시는 분을 믿는 사람에게는 그의 믿음이 의로 여겨집니다.

v.CNV: 大卫也是这样说，那不靠行为而蒙　神算为义的人是有福的！
v.ESV: just as David also speaks of the blessing of the one to whom God counts righteousness apart from works:
v.GAE: 일한 것이 없이 하나님께 의로 여기심을 받는 사람의 복에 대하여 다윗이 말한 바
v.KJV: Even as David also describeth the blessedness of the man, unto whom God imputeth righteousness without works,
v.NASB: just as David also speaks of the blessing on the man to whom God credits righteousness apart from works:
v.NIV: David says the same thing when he speaks of the blessedness of the one to whom God credits righteousness apart from works:
v.NRSV: So also David speaks of the blessedness of those to whom God reckons righteousness apart from works:
v.SAENEW: 그래서 행한 것이 없어도, 하나님께서 의롭다고 여겨 주시는 사람이 받을 복을 다윗도 다음과 같이 말하였습니다.
v.WLB: 행위와 상관없이 하나님께 의롭다고 인정받는 사람의 복에 대해 다윗도 이렇게 말합니다.

v.CNV: “过犯得蒙赦免，罪恶得到遮盖的人，是有福的；
v.ESV: “Blessed are those whose lawless deeds are forgiven, and whose sins are covered;
v.GAE: 불법이 사함을 받고 죄가 가리어짐을 받는 사람들은 복이 있고
v.KJV: Saying, Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered.
v.NASB: 'BLESSED ARE THOSE WHOSE LAWLESS DEEDS HAVE BEEN FORGIVEN, AND WHOSE SINS HAVE BEEN COVERED.
v.NIV: “Blessed are those whose transgressions are forgiven, whose sins are covered.
v.NRSV: “Blessed are those whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered;
v.SAENEW: "하나님께서 잘못을 용서해 주시고 죄를 덮어 주신 사람은 복이 있다.
v.WLB: “주께서 불법을 용서하시고 죄를 덮어 주시는 사람은 복이 있고

v.CNV: 主不算为有罪的，这人是有福的。”
v.ESV: blessed is the man against whom the Lord will not count his sin.”
v.GAE: 주께서 그 죄를 인정하지 아니하실 사람은 복이 있도다 함과 같으니라
v.KJV: Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin.
v.NASB: 'BLESSED IS THE MAN WHOSE SIN THE LORD WILL NOT TAKE INTO ACCOUNT.'
v.NIV: Blessed is the one whose sin the Lord will never count against them.”
v.NRSV: blessed is the one against whom the Lord will not reckon sin.”
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 죄 없다고 인정해 주실 사람은 복이 있다."
v.WLB: 주께서 그 죄를 인정치 않으실 사람은 복이 있다.”

v.CNV: 这样看来，那有福的人，是指受割礼的呢，还是指没有受割礼的呢？因为我们说：“亚伯拉罕的信算为他的义。”
v.ESV: Is this blessing then only for the circumcised, or also for the uncircumcised? For we say that faith was counted to Abraham as righteousness.
v.GAE: 그런즉 이 복이 할례자에게냐 혹은 무할례자에게도냐 무릇 우리가 말하기를 아브라함에게는 그 믿음이 의로 여겨졌다 하노라
v.KJV: Cometh this blessedness then upon the circumcision only, or upon the uncircumcision also? for we say that faith was reckoned to Abraham for righteousness.
v.NASB: Is this blessing then on the circumcised, or on the uncircumcised also? For we say, 'FAITH WAS CREDITED TO ABRAHAM AS RIGHTEOUSNESS.'
v.NIV: Is this blessedness only for the circumcised, or also for the uncircumcised? We have been saying that Abraham’s faith was credited to him as righteousness.
v.NRSV: Is this blessedness, then, pronounced only on the circumcised, or also on the uncircumcised? We say, “Faith was reckoned to Abraham as righteousness.”
v.SAENEW: 그러면 이러한 복은 할례를 받은 사람에게만 내리는 것입니까? 그렇지 않으면 할례를 받지 않은 사람에게도 내리는 것입니까? 우리는 앞에서 말하기를 "하나님께서 아브라함의 믿음을 의로 여기셨다" 하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그러면 이 복은 할례받은 사람에게만 내리는 것입니까? 아니면 할례받지 않은 사람에게도 내리는 것입니까? 우리가 말하기를 “아브라함이 하나님을 믿으니 이것이 그에게 의로 여겨졌다”라고 했습니다.

v.CNV: 那么，是怎样算的呢？是在他受割礼以后呢，还是在他受割礼之前呢？不是在他受割礼以后，而是在他受了割礼以前。
v.ESV: How then was it counted to him? Was it before or after he had been circumcised? It was not after, but before he was circumcised.
v.GAE: 그런즉 그것이 어떻게 여겨졌느냐 할례시냐 무할례시냐 할례시가 아니요 무할례시니라
v.KJV: How was it then reckoned? when he was in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.
v.NASB: How then was it credited? While he was circumcised, or uncircumcised? Not while circumcised, but while uncircumcised;
v.NIV: Under what circumstances was it credited? Was it after he was circumcised, or before? It was not after, but before!
v.NRSV: How then was it reckoned to him? Was it before or after he had been circumcised? It was not after, but before he was circumcised.
v.SAENEW: 그러면 어떻게 아브라함이 그러한 인정을 받았습니까? 그가 할례를 받은 후에 그렇게 되었습니까? 그렇지 않으면 할례를 받기 전에 그렇게 되었습니까? 그것은 할례를 받은 후에 된 일이 아니라, 할례를 받기 전에 된 일입니다.
v.WLB: 그러면 이것이 어떻게 의로 여겨졌습니까? 그가 할례를 받은 때입니까? 아니면 할례를 받지 않은 때입니까? 할례를 받은 때가 아니라 할례를 받지 않은 때입니다.

v.CNV: 他领受了割礼为记号，作他受割礼之前因信称义的印证，使他作所有没有受割礼而信之人的父，使他们也被算为义；
v.ESV: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. The purpose was to make him the father of all who believe without being circumcised, so that righteousness would be counted to them as well,
v.GAE: 그가 할례의 표를 받은 것은 무할례시에 믿음으로 된 의를 인친 것이니 이는 무할례자로서 믿는 모든 자의 조상이 되어 그들도 의로 여기심을 얻게 하려 하심이라
v.KJV: And he received the sign of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had yet being uncircumcised: that he might be the father of all them that believe, though they be not circumcised; that righteousness might be imputed unto them also:
v.NASB: and he received the sign of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had while uncircumcised, so that he might be the father of all who believe without being circumcised, that righteousness might be credited to them,
v.NIV: And he received circumcision as a sign, a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. So then, he is the father of all who believe but have not been circumcised, in order that righteousness might be credited to them.
v.NRSV: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. The purpose was to make him the ancestor of all who believe without being circumcised and who thus have righteousness reckoned to them,
v.SAENEW: 아브라함이 할례라는 표를 받았는데, 그것은 그가 할례를 받지 않은 상태에서 이미 얻은 믿음의 의를 확증하는 것이었습니다. 그래서 그는 할례를 받지 않고도 믿는 모든 사람의 조상이 되었으니, 이것은 할례를 받지 않은 사람들도 의롭다는 인정을 받게 하려는 것이었습니다.
v.WLB: 그가 할례의 표식을 받은 것은 할례를 받지 않은 때에 얻은 믿음의 의를 확증하는 것이었습니다. 이는 아브라함이 할례받지 않은 사람으로서 믿는 모든 사람들의 조상이 돼 그들도 의롭다는 인정을 받게 하려는 것이었습니다.

v.CNV: 又作受割礼之人的父，就是作那些不仅受割礼，并且照着我们祖宗亚伯拉罕未受割礼时就信的那脚踪而行之人的父。
v.ESV: and to make him the father of the circumcised who are not merely circumcised but who also walk in the footsteps of the faith that our father Abraham had before he was circumcised.
v.GAE: 또한 할례자의 조상이 되었나니 곧 할례 받을 자에게뿐 아니라 우리 조상 아브라함이 무할례시에 가졌던 믿음의 자취를 따르는 자들에게도 그러하니라
v.KJV: And the father of circumcision to them who are not of the circumcision only, but who also walk in the steps of that faith of our father Abraham, which he had being yet uncircumcised.
v.NASB: and the father of circumcision to those who not only are of the circumcision, but who also follow in the steps of the faith of our father Abraham which he had while uncircumcised.
v.NIV: And he is then also the father of the circumcised who not only are circumcised but who also follow in the footsteps of the faith that our father Abraham had before he was circumcised.
v.NRSV: and likewise the ancestor of the circumcised who are not only circumcised but who also follow the example of the faith that our ancestor Abraham had before he was circumcised.
v.SAENEW: 또 그는 할례를 받은 사람의 조상이 되기도 하였습니다. 다시 말하면, 할례만을 받은 것이 아니라 또한 우리 조상 아브라함이 할례를 받지 않은 상태에서 걸어간 믿음의 발자취를 따라가는 사람들의 조상이 되었습니다.
v.WLB: 그는 또한 할례받은 사람의 조상이 됐습니다. 곧 할례를 받았을 뿐 아니라 우리 조상 아브라함이 할례를 받지 않은 때에 가졌던 믿음의 발자취를 따르는 사람들의 조상이 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 原来　神给亚伯拉罕和他后裔承受世界的应许，并不是因着律法，而是借着因信而来的义。
v.ESV: For the promise to Abraham and his offspring that he would be heir of the world did not come through the law but through the righteousness of faith.
v.GAE: 아브라함이나 그 후손에게 세상의 상속자가 되리라고 하신 언약은 율법으로 말미암은 것이 아니요 오직 믿음의 의로 말미암은 것이니라
v.KJV: For the promise, that he should be the heir of the world, was not to Abraham, or to his seed, through the law, but through the righteousness of faith.
v.NASB: For the promise to Abraham or to his descendants that he would be heir of the world was not through the Law, but through the righteousness of faith.
v.NIV: It was not through the law that Abraham and his offspring received the promise that he would be heir of the world, but through the righteousness that comes by faith.
v.NRSV: For the promise that he would inherit the world did not come to Abraham or to his descendants through the law but through the righteousness of faith.
v.SAENEW: 아브라함이나 그 자손에게 주신 하나님의 약속, 곧 그들이 세상을 물려받을 상속자가 되리라는 것은, 율법으로 말미암은 것이 아니라, 믿음의 의로 말미암은 것입니다.
v.WLB: 아브라함이나 그의 후손에게 세상의 상속자가 되리라고 하신 약속은 율법으로 인해 된 것이 아니라 오직 믿음의 의로 인해 된 것입니다.

v.CNV: 如果属于律法的人才能成为后嗣，信就没有作用，应许也就落空了。
v.ESV: For if it is the adherents of the law who are to be the heirs, faith is null and the promise is void.
v.GAE: 만일 율법에 속한 자들이 상속자이면 믿음은 헛것이 되고 약속은 파기되었느니라
v.KJV: For if they which are of the law be heirs, faith is made void, and the promise made of none effect:
v.NASB: For if those who are of the Law are heirs, faith is made void and the promise is nullified;
v.NIV: For if those who depend on the law are heirs, faith means nothing and the promise is worthless,
v.NRSV: If it is the adherents of the law who are to be the heirs, faith is null and the promise is void.
v.SAENEW: 율법을 의지하는 사람들이 상속자가 된다면, 믿음은 무의미한 것이 되고, 약속은 헛된 것이 됩니다.
v.WLB: 만일 율법을 따라 사는 사람들이 상속자가 된다면 믿음은 무효가 되고 약속은 파기됐을 것입니다.

v.CNV: 因为律法带来刑罚，没有律法，就没有违背律法的事。
v.ESV: For the law brings wrath, but where there is no law there is no transgression.
v.GAE: 율법은 진노를 이루게 하나니 율법이 없는 곳에는 범법도 없느니라
v.KJV: Because the law worketh wrath: for where no law is, there is no transgression.
v.NASB: for the Law brings about wrath, but where there is no law, there also is no violation.
v.NIV: because the law brings wrath. And where there is no law there is no transgression.
v.NRSV: For the law brings wrath; but where there is no law, neither is there violation.
v.SAENEW: 율법은 진노를 불러옵니다. 율법이 없는 곳에는 범법도 없습니다.
v.WLB: 왜냐하면 율법은 진노를 부르기 때문입니다. 율법이 없는 곳에는 범법함도 없습니다.

v.CNV: 所以，成为后嗣是因着信，为的是要按着　神的恩典，使给所有后裔的应许坚定不移，不但临到那属于律法的人，也临到那效法亚伯拉罕而信的人。亚伯拉罕在　神面前作我们众人的父，
v.ESV: That is why it depends on faith, in order that the promise may rest on grace and be guaranteed to all his offspring—not only to the adherent of the law but also to the one who shares the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all,
v.GAE: 그러므로 상속자가 되는 그것이 은혜에 속하기 위하여 믿음으로 되나니 이는 그 약속을 그 모든 후손에게 굳게 하려 하심이라 율법에 속한 자에게뿐만 아니라 아브라함의 믿음에 속한 자에게도 그러하니 아브라함은 우리 모든 사람의 조상이라
v.KJV: Therefore it is of faith, that it might be by grace; to the end the promise might be sure to all the seed; not to that only which is of the law, but to that also which is of the faith of Abraham; who is the father of us all,
v.NASB: For this reason it is by faith, in order that it may be in accordance with grace, so that the promise will be guaranteed to all the descendants, not only to those who are of the Law, but also to those who are of the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all,
v.NIV: Therefore, the promise comes by faith, so that it may be by grace and may be guaranteed to all Abraham’s offspring—not only to those who are of the law but also to those who have the faith of Abraham. He is the father of us all.
v.NRSV: For this reason it depends on faith, in order that the promise may rest on grace and be guaranteed to all his descendants, not only to the adherents of the law but also to those who share the faith of Abraham (for he is the father of all of us,
v.SAENEW: 이런 까닭에, 이 약속은 믿음에 근거한 것입니다. 그것은 하나님께서 아브라함에게 이 약속을 은혜로 주셔서 이것을 그의 모든 후손에게도, 곧 율법으로 사는 사람들에게만이 아니라 아브라함이 지닌 믿음으로 사는 사람들에게도 보장하시려는 것입니다. 아브라함은 우리 모두의 조상입니다.
v.WLB: 그러므로 약속은 믿음으로 말미암습니다. 이는 아브라함의 모든 후손, 곧 율법을 따라 사는 사람뿐 아니라 아브라함의 믿음을 따라 사는 사람들에게도 은혜로 이 약속을 보장해 주시기 위한 것입니다. 아브라함은 우리 모두의 조상입니다.

v.CNV: 如经上所记：“我已经立了你作万国的父。”他所信的，是叫死人得生命、使无变为有的　神。
v.ESV: as it is written, “I have made you the father of many nations”—in the presence of the God in whom he believed, who gives life to the dead and calls into existence the things that do not exist.
v.GAE: 기록된 바 내가 너를 많은 민족의 조상으로 세웠다 하심과 같으니 그가 믿은 바 하나님은 죽은 자를 살리시며 없는 것을 있는 것으로 부르시는 이시니라
v.KJV: (As it is written, I have made thee a father of many nations,) before him whom he believed, even God, who quickeneth the dead, and calleth those things which be not as though they were.
v.NASB: (as it is written, 'A FATHER OF MANY NATIONS HAVE I MADE YOU') in the presence of Him whom he believed, even God, who gives life to the dead and calls into being that which does not exist.
v.NIV: As it is written: “I have made you a father of many nations.” He is our father in the sight of God, in whom he believed—the God who gives life to the dead and calls into being things that were not.
v.NRSV: as it is written, “I have made you the father of many nations”) — in the presence of the God in whom he believed, who gives life to the dead and calls into existence the things that do not exist.
v.SAENEW: 이것은 성경에 기록된 대로 "내가 너를 많은 민족의 조상으로 세웠다" 함과 같습니다. 이 약속은, 그가 믿은 하나님, 다시 말하면, 죽은 사람들을 살리시며 없는 것들을 불러내어 있는 것이 되게 하시는 하나님께서 보장하신 것입니다.
v.WLB: 기록되기를 “내가 너를 많은 민족의 조상으로 세웠다”라고 한 것과 같습니다. 아브라함은 그가 믿은 하나님, 곧 죽은 사람을 살리시며 없는 것을 있는 것같이 부르시는 하나님 앞에서 우리의 조상이 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 在他没有盼望的时候，仍然怀着盼望去信，因此就成了万国的父，正如　神所说：“你的后裔将要这样众多。”
v.ESV: In hope he believed against hope, that he should become the father of many nations, as he had been told, “So shall your offspring be.”
v.GAE: 아브라함이 바랄 수 없는 중에 바라고 믿었으니 이는 네 후손이 이같으리라 하신 말씀대로 많은 민족의 조상이 되게 하려 하심이라
v.KJV: Who against hope believed in hope, that he might become the father of many nations, according to that which was spoken, So shall thy seed be.
v.NASB: In hope against hope he believed, so that he might become a father of many nations according to that which had been spoken, 'SO SHALL YOUR DESCENDANTS BE.'
v.NIV: Against all hope, Abraham in hope believed and so became the father of many nations, just as it had been said to him, “So shall your offspring be.”
v.NRSV: Hoping against hope, he believed that he would become “the father of many nations,” according to what was said, “So numerous shall your descendants be.”
v.SAENEW: 아브라함은 희망이 사라진 때에도 바라면서 믿었으므로 "너의 자손이 이와 같이 많아질 것이다" 하신 말씀대로, 많은 민족의 조상이 되었습니다.
v.WLB: 아브라함은 소망이 없는 가운데서도 소망을 갖고 믿었습니다. 이는 “네 후손이 이와 같을 것이다”라고 하신 말씀대로 많은 민족의 조상이 되게 하기 위한 것이었습니다.

v.CNV: 他快到一百岁的时候，想到自己的身体好像已经死了，撒拉也不能生育，他的信心还是不软弱，
v.ESV: He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body, which was as good as dead ( since he was about a hundred years old), or when he considered the barrenness of Sarah's womb.
v.GAE: 그가 백 세나 되어 자기 몸이 죽은 것 같고 사라의 태가 죽은 것 같음을 알고도 믿음이 약하여지지 아니하고
v.KJV: And being not weak in faith, he considered not his own body now dead, when he was about an hundred years old, neither yet the deadness of Sarah’s womb:
v.NASB: Without becoming weak in faith he contemplated his own body, now as good as dead since he was about a hundred years old, and the deadness of Sarah's womb;
v.NIV: Without weakening in his faith, he faced the fact that his body was as good as dead —since he was about a hundred years old —and that Sarah’s womb was also dead.
v.NRSV: He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body, which was already as good as dead (for he was about a hundred years old), or when he considered the barrenness of Sarah’s womb.
v.SAENEW: 그는 나이가 백 세가 되어서, 자기 몸이 [이미] 죽은 것이나 다름없고, 또한 사라의 태도 죽은 것이나 다름없는 줄 알면서도, 그는 믿음이 약해지지 않았습니다.
v.WLB: 아브라함은 100세나 돼 이미 자기 몸이 죽은 것 같고 사라의 태가 죽은 것 같음을 알고도 믿음이 약해지지 않았습니다.

v.CNV: 也没有因着不信而疑惑　神的应许，反倒坚信不移，把荣耀归给　神，
v.ESV: No unbelief made him waver concerning the promise of God, but he grew strong in his faith as he gave glory to God,
v.GAE: 믿음이 없어 하나님의 약속을 의심하지 않고 믿음으로 견고하여져서 하나님께 영광을 돌리며
v.KJV: He staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God;
v.NASB: yet, with respect to the promise of God, he did not waver in unbelief but grew strong in faith, giving glory to God,
v.NIV: Yet he did not waver through unbelief regarding the promise of God, but was strengthened in his faith and gave glory to God,
v.NRSV: No distrust made him waver concerning the promise of God, but he grew strong in his faith as he gave glory to God,
v.SAENEW: 그는 하나님의 약속을 믿고 의심하지 않았습니다. 오히려 그는 믿음이 굳세어져서 하나님께 영광을 돌렸습니다.
v.WLB: 그는 하나님의 약속을 믿고 의심하지 않았고 도리어 믿음이 굳건해져서 하나님께 영광을 돌리고

v.CNV: 满心相信　神所应许的，　神必能成就。
v.ESV: fully convinced that God was able to do what he had promised.
v.GAE: 약속하신 그것을 또한 능히 이루실 줄을 확신하였으니
v.KJV: And being fully persuaded that, what he had promised, he was able also to perform.
v.NASB: and being fully assured that what God had promised, He was able also to perform.
v.NIV: being fully persuaded that God had power to do what he had promised.
v.NRSV: being fully convinced that God was able to do what he had promised.
v.SAENEW: 그는, 하나님께서 스스로 약속하신 바를 능히 이루실 것이라고 확신하였습니다.
v.WLB: 약속하신 그것을 또한 능히 이루실 것을 확신했습니다.

v.CNV: 因此，这就算为他的义。
v.ESV: That is why his faith was “counted to him as righteousness.”
v.GAE: 그러므로 그것이 그에게 의로 여겨졌느니라
v.KJV: And therefore it was imputed to him for righteousness.
v.NASB: Therefore IT WAS ALSO CREDITED TO HIM AS RIGHTEOUSNESS.
v.NIV: This is why “it was credited to him as righteousness.”
v.NRSV: Therefore his faith “was reckoned to him as righteousness.”
v.SAENEW: 그래서 하나님께서는 이것을 보시고 "그를 의롭다고 여겨 주셨습니다."
v.WLB: 그러므로 이것이 그에게 의로 여겨졌습니다.

v.CNV: “算为他的义”这一句话，不是单为他写的，
v.ESV: But the words “it was counted to him” were not written for his sake alone,
v.GAE: 그에게 의로 여겨졌다 기록된 것은 아브라함만 위한 것이 아니요
v.KJV: Now it was not written for his sake alone, that it was imputed to him;
v.NASB: Now not for his sake only was it written that it was credited to him,
v.NIV: The words “it was credited to him” were written not for him alone,
v.NRSV: Now the words, “it was reckoned to him,” were written not for his sake alone,
v.SAENEW: "그가 의롭다는 인정을 받았다" 하는 말은, 아브라함만을 위하여 기록된 것이 아니라,
v.WLB: “그에게 의로 여겨졌다”라는 말은 아브라함만을 위해 기록된 것이 아니라

v.CNV: 也是为我们这将来得算为义的人写的，就是为我们这信　神使我们的主耶稣从死人中复活的人写的。
v.ESV: but for ours also. It will be counted to us who believe in him who raised from the dead Jesus our Lord,
v.GAE: 의로 여기심을 받을 우리도 위함이니 곧 예수 우리 주를 죽은 자 가운데서 살리신 이를 믿는 자니라
v.KJV: But for us also, to whom it shall be imputed, if we believe on him that raised up Jesus our Lord from the dead;
v.NASB: but for our sake also, to whom it will be credited, as those who believe in Him who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead,
v.NIV: but also for us, to whom God will credit righteousness—for us who believe in him who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead.
v.NRSV: but for ours also. It will be reckoned to us who believe in him who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead,
v.SAENEW: 하나님께서 의롭다고 여겨 주실 우리, 곧 우리 주 예수를 죽은 사람들 가운데서 살리신 분을 믿는 우리까지도 위한 것입니다.
v.WLB: 의롭다는 인정을 받을 우리, 곧 우리 주 예수를 죽은 사람 가운데서 살리신 분을 믿는 우리도 위한 것입니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣为我们的过犯被交去处死，为我们的称义而复活。
v.ESV: who was delivered up for our trespasses and raised for our justification.
v.GAE: 예수는 우리가 범죄한 것 때문에 내줌이 되고 또한 우리를 의롭다 하시기 위하여 살아나셨느니라
v.KJV: Who was delivered for our offences, and was raised again for our justification.
v.NASB: He who was delivered over because of our transgressions, and was raised because of our justification.
v.NIV: He was delivered over to death for our sins and was raised to life for our justification.
v.NRSV: who was handed over to death for our trespasses and was raised for our justification.
v.SAENEW: 예수는 우리의 범죄 때문에 죽임을 당하셨고, 우리를 의롭게 하시려고 살아나셨습니다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 우리의 범죄를 인해 죽음에 넘겨지셨고 우리의 의를 위해 살리심을 받았습니다.
